subcommands.name: install
subcommands.description: Install a chart

options.name: --atomic
options.description: If set, the installation process deletes the installation on failure. The --wait flag will be set automatically if --atomic is used

options.name: --ca-file
options.description: Verify certificates of HTTPS-enabled servers using this CA bundle

options.name: --cert-file
options.description: Identify HTTPS client using this SSL certificate file

options.name: --create-namespace
options.description: Create the release namespace if not present

options.name: --dependency-update
options.description: Update dependencies if they are missing before installing the chart

options.name: --description
options.description: Add a custom description

options.name: --devel
options.description: Use development versions, too. Equivalent to version '>0.0.0-0'. If --version is set, this is ignored

options.name: --disable-openapi-validation
options.description: If set, the installation process will not validate rendered templates against the Kubernetes OpenAPI Schema

options.name: --dry-run
options.description: Simulate an install

name: --generate-name
-g
options.description: Generate the name (and omit the NAME parameter

options.name: --insecure-skip-tls-verify
options.description: Skip tls certificate checks for the chart download

options.name: --key-file
options.description: Identify HTTPS client using this SSL key file

options.name: --keyring
options.description: Location of public keys used for verification (default "~/.gnupg/pubring.gpg")

options.name: --name-template
options.description: Specify template used to name the release

options.name: --no-hooks
options.description: Prevent hooks from running during install

name: --output
-o
options.description: Prints the output in the specified format. Allowed values: table, json, yaml (default table)

options.name: --pass-credentials
options.description: Pass credentials to all domains

options.name: --password
options.description: Chart repository password where to locate the requested chart

options.name: --post-renderer
options.description: The path to an executable to be used for post rendering. If it exists in $PATH, the binary will be used, otherwise it will try to look for the executable at the given path (default exec)

options.name: --render-subchart-notes
options.description: If set, render subchart notes along with the parent

options.name: --replace
options.description: Re-use the given name, only if that name is a deleted release which remains in the history. This is unsafe in production

options.name: --repo
options.description: Chart repository url where to locate the requested chart

options.name: --set
options.description: Set values on the command line (can specify multiple or separate values with commas: key1=val1,key2=val2)

options.name: --set-file
options.description: Set values from respective files specified via the command line (can specify multiple or separate values with commas: key1=path1,key2=path2)

options.name: --set-string
options.description: Set STRING values on the command line (can specify multiple or separate values with commas: key1=val1,key2=val2)

options.name: --skip-crds
options.description: If set, no CRDs will be installed. By default, CRDs are installed if not already present

options.name: --timeout
options.description: Time to wait for any individual Kubernetes operation (like Jobs for hooks) (default 5m0s)

name: --values
-f
options.description: Specify values in a YAML file or a URL (can specify multiple)

options.name: --username
options.description: Chart repository username where to locate the requested chart

options.name: --verify
options.description: Verify the package before using it

options.name: --version
options.description: Specify a version constraint for the chart version to use. This constraint can be a specific tag (e.g. 1.1.1) or it may reference a valid range (e.g. ^2.0.0). If this is not specified, the latest version is used

options.name: --wait
options.description: If set, will wait until all Pods, PVCs, Services, and minimum number of Pods of a Deployment, StatefulSet, or ReplicaSet are in a ready state before marking the release as successful. It will wait for as long as --timeout

options.name: --wait-for-jobs
options.description: If set and --wait enabled, will wait until all Jobs have been completed before marking the release as successful. It will wait for as long as --timeout

subcommands.name: lint
subcommands.description: Examine a chart for possible issues

options.name: --set
options.description: Set values on the command line (can specify multiple or separate values with commas: key1=val1,key2=val2)

options.name: --set-file
options.description: Set values from respective files specified via the command line (can specify multiple or separate values with commas: key1=path1,key2=path2)

options.name: --set-string
options.description: Set STRING values on the command line (can specify multiple or separate values with commas: key1=val1,key2=val2)

options.name: --strict
options.description: Fail on lint warnings

name: --values
-f
options.description: Specify values in a YAML file or a URL (can specify multiple)

options.name: --with-subcharts
options.description: Lint dependent charts

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: List releases

name: --all
-a
options.description: Show all releases without any filter applied

name: --all-namespaces
-A
options.description: List releases across all namespaces

name: --date
-d
options.description: Sort by release date

options.name: --deployed
options.description: Show deployed releases. If no other is specified, this will be automatically enabled

options.name: --failed
options.description: Show failed releases

name: --filter
-f
options.description: A regular expression (Perl compatible). Any releases that match the expression will be included in the results

options.name: --max
options.description: Maximum number of releases to fetch (default 256)

options.name: --offset
options.description: Next release index in the list, used to offset from start value

name: --output
-o
options.description: Prints the output in the specified format. Allowed values: table, json, yaml (default table)

options.name: --pending
options.description: Show pending releases

name: --reverse
-r
options.description: Reverse the sort order

name: --selector
-l
options.description: Selector (label query) to filter on, supports '=', '==', and '!='.(e.g. -l key1=value1,key2=value2). Works only for secret(default) and configmap storage backends

name: --short
-q
options.description: Output short (quiet) listing format

options.name: --superseded
options.description: Show superseded releases

options.name: --time-format
options.description: Format time using golang time formatter. Example: --time-format "2006-01-02 15:04:05Z0700"

options.name: --uninstalled
options.description: Show uninstalled releases (if 'helm uninstall --keep-history' was used)

options.name: --uninstalling
options.description: Show releases that are currently being uninstalled

options.description: Set the appVersion on the chart to this version

-u

name: --destination
-d
options.description: Location to write the chart. (default ".")

options.name: --key
options.description: Name of the key to use when signing. Used if --sign is true

options.name: --keyring
options.description: Location of public keys used for verification (default "~/.gnupg/pubring.gpg")

options.name: --passphrase-file
options.description: Location of a file which contains the passphrase for the signing key. Use "-" in order to read from stdin

options.name: --sign
options.description: Use a PGP private key to sign this package

options.name: --version
options.description: Set the version on the chart to this version

options.name: --render-subchart-notes
options.description: If set, render subchart notes along with the parent

options.name: --replace
options.description: Re-use the given name, only if that name is a deleted release which remains in the history. This is unsafe in production

options.name: --repo
options.description: Chart repository url where to locate the requested chart

options.name: --set
options.description: Set values on the command line (can specify multiple or separate values with commas: key1=val1,key2=val2)

options.name: --set-file
options.description: Set values from respective files specified via the command line (can specify multiple or separate values with commas: key1=path1,key2=path2)

options.name: --set-string
options.description: Set STRING values on the command line (can specify multiple or separate values with commas: key1=val1,key2=val2)

name: --show-only
-s
options.description: Only show manifests rendered from the given templates

options.name: --skip-crds
options.description: If set, no CRDs will be installed. By default, CRDs are installed if not already present

options.name: --skip-tests
options.description: Skip tests from templated output

options.name: --timeout
options.description: Time to wait for any individual Kubernetes operation (like Jobs for hooks) (default 5m0s)

options.name: --username
options.description: Chart repository username where to locate the requested chart

options.name: --validate
options.description: Validate your manifests against the Kubernetes cluster you are currently pointing at. This is the same validation performed on an install

name: --values
-f
options.description: Specify values in a YAML file or a URL (can specify multiple)

options.name: --verify
options.description: Verify the package before using it

options.name: --version
options.description: Specify a version constraint for the chart version to use. This constraint can be a specific tag (e.g. 1.1.1) or it may reference a valid range (e.g. ^2.0.0). If this is not specified, the latest version is used

options.name: --wait
options.description: If set, will wait until all Pods, PVCs, Services, and minimum number of Pods of a Deployment, StatefulSet, or ReplicaSet are in a ready state before marking the release as successful. It will wait for as long as --timeout

options.name: --wait-for-jobs
options.description: If set and --wait enabled, will wait until all Jobs have been completed before marking the release as successful. It will wait for as long as --timeout

subcommands.name: test
subcommands.description: Run tests for a release

options.name: --filter
options.description: Specify tests by attribute (currently "name") using attribute=value syntax or '!attribute=value' to exclude a test (can specify multiple or separate values with commas: name=test1,name=test2)

options.name: --logs
options.description: Dump the logs from test pods (this runs after all tests are complete, but before any cleanup)

options.name: --timeout
options.description: Time to wait for any individual Kubernetes operation (like Jobs for hooks) (default 5m0s)

subcommands.name: uninstall
subcommands.description: Uninstall a release

options.name: --description
options.description: Add a custom description

options.name: --dry-run
options.description: Simulate an install

options.name: --keep-history
options.description: Remove all associated resources and mark the release as deleted, but retain the release history

options.name: --no-hooks
options.description: Prevent hooks from running during install

options.name: --timeout
options.description: Time to wait for any individual Kubernetes operation (like Jobs for hooks) (default 5m0s)

options.name: --wait
options.description: If set, will wait until all Pods, PVCs, Services, and minimum number of Pods of a Deployment, StatefulSet, or ReplicaSet are in a ready state before marking the release as successful. It will wait for as long as --timeout

subcommands.name: upgrade
subcommands.description: Upgrade a release

options.name: --atomic
options.description: If set, the installation process deletes the installation on failure. The --wait flag will be set automatically if --atomic is used

options.name: --ca-file
options.description: Verify certificates of HTTPS-enabled servers using this CA bundle

options.name: --cert-file
options.description: Identify HTTPS client using this SSL certificate file

options.name: --cleanup-on-fail
options.description: Allow deletion of new resources created in this rollback when rollback fails

options.name: --create-namespace
options.description: Create the release namespace if not present

options.name: --dependency-update
options.description: Update dependencies if they are missing before installing the chart

options.name: --description
options.description: Add a custom description

options.name: --devel
options.description: Use development versions, too. Equivalent to version '>0.0.0-0'. If --version is set, this is ignored

options.name: --disable-openapi-validation
options.description: If set, the installation process will not validate rendered templates against the Kubernetes OpenAPI Schema

options.name: --dry-run
options.description: Simulate an install

options.name: --force
options.description: Force resource update through delete/recreate if needed

options.name: --history-max
options.description: Limit the maximum number of revisions saved per release. Use 0 for no limit (default 10)

options.name: --insecure-skip-tls-verify
options.description: Skip tls certificate checks for the chart download

name: --install
-i
options.description: If a release by this name doesn't already exist, run an install

options.name: --key-file
options.description: Identify HTTPS client using this SSL key file

options.name: --keyring
options.description: Location of public keys used for verification (default "~/.gnupg/pubring.gpg")

options.name: --no-hooks
options.description: Prevent hooks from running during install

name: --output
-o
options.description: Prints the output in the specified format. Allowed values: table, json, yaml (default table)

options.name: --pass-credentials
options.description: Pass credentials to all domains

options.name: --password
options.description: Chart repository password where to locate the requested chart

options.name: --post-renderer
options.description: The path to an executable to be used for post rendering. If it exists in $PATH, the binary will be used, otherwise it will try to look for the executable at the given path (default exec)

options.name: --render-subchart-notes
options.description: If set, render subchart notes along with the parent

options.name: --repo
options.description: Chart repository url where to locate the requested chart

options.name: --reset-values
options.description: When upgrading, reset the values to the ones built into the chart

options.name: --reuse-values
options.description: When upgrading, reuse the last release's values and merge in any overrides from the command line via --set and -f. If '--reset-values' is specified, this is ignored

options.description: Set values on the command line (can specify multiple or separate values with commas: key1=val1,key2=val2)

options.description: If set, no CRDs will be installed. By default, CRDs are installed if not already present

-f
options.description: Specify values in a YAML file or a URL (can specify multiple)

options.description: Verify the package before using it

options.description: Specify a version constraint for the chart version to use. This constraint can be a specific tag (e.g. 1.1.1) or it may reference a valid range (e.g. ^2.0.0). If this is not specified, the latest version is used

options.description: If set, will wait until all Pods, PVCs, Services, and minimum number of Pods of a Deployment, StatefulSet, or ReplicaSet are in a ready state before marking the release as successful. It will wait for as long as --timeout

subcommands.name: verify
subcommands.description: Verify that a chart at the given path has been signed and is valid

options.description: Location of public keys used for verification (default "~/.gnupg/pubring.gpg")

subcommands.name: version
subcommands.description: Print the client version information

options.name: --short
options.description: Print the version number

options.name: --template
options.description: Template for version string format

name: --help
-h
options.description: Show help for helm

options.name: --debug
options.description: Enable verbose output

options.name: --kube-apiserver
options.description: The address and the port for the Kubernetes API server

options.name: --kube-as-group
options.description: Group to impersonate for the operation, this flag can be repeated to specify multiple groups

options.name: --kube-as-user
options.description: Username to impersonate for the operation

options.name: --kube-ca-file
options.description: The certificate authority file for the Kubernetes API server connection

options.name: --kube-context
options.description: Name of the kubeconfig context to use

options.name: --kube-token
options.description: Bearer token used for authentication

options.name: --kubeconfig
options.description: Path to the kubeconfig file

name: --namespace
-n
options.description: Namespace scope for this request

options.name: --registry-config
options.description: Path to the registry config file

options.name: --repository-cache
options.description: Path to the file containing cached repository indexes

options.name: --repository-config
options.description: Path to the file containing repository names and URLs
